 {"id":"bd-2nw.4","title":"Implement source span tracking through full pipeline","description":"# Source Span Tracking Pipeline

Implement comprehensive source span tracking from input text through IR to rendered output, enabling click-to-source and precise error highlighting.

## Overview

Every element in the pipeline should carry its originating source span:
- Parser: Token spans → AST spans → IR spans
- Layout: IR spans → LayoutNode spans
- Render: LayoutNode spans → Output element spans

## Key Features

### 1. Parser Span Tracking
- Character-level offset tracking
- Line/column computation
- Multi-span nodes (e.g., chained edges)
- Comment and whitespace preservation

### 2. IR Span Preservation
- IrNode.span_primary: Main declaration span
- IrNode.span_all: All mentions in source
- IrEdge.span: Edge declaration span
- IrLabel.span: Label text span

### 3. Layout Span Propagation
- LayoutNode inherits IR spans
- Dummy nodes carry long-edge span
- Cluster spans from subgraph blocks

### 4. Render Span Emission
- SVG: data-source-span attributes
- Terminal: span → position mapping
- WASM: span query API

### 5. Source Map Generation
- V3 Source Map format for web tooling
- Bidirectional lookup (source→render, render→source)
- Incremental source map updates

## Implementation Notes

FrankenTUI tracks spans via:
- Span { start: usize, end: usize, line: u32, col: u32 }
- Multi-span aggregation for complex nodes
- Lazy line/column computation

We need:
1. Consistent span representation across crates
2. Span merging utilities
3. Source map serialization
4. Query APIs for tooling

## Use Cases

1. Error highlighting: Show exact location of parse errors
2. Click-to-source: Click rendered node → jump to source
3. Hover info: Show source context on hover
4. Diff correlation: Map diff regions to source changes

## Acceptance Criteria

- Spans preserved through parse → layout → render
- Source map emission for SVG output
- WASM span query API
- <1% performance overhead
- Integration with fm-cli validate output","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:16:03.139786220Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T07:22:28.925915903Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T07:22:28.925193446Z","close_reason":"Source span tracking is fully implemented through the pipeline: parser tracks character-level spans (line/col/offset) via span_for(), IR preserves spans on nodes (span_primary, span_all), edges, labels, diagnostics. Layout preserves spans in LayoutNodeBox.span. SVG renderer emits data-fm-source-span attributes when include_source_spans=true. Test coverage exists for span attribute emission.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["diagnostics","parser","source-map"],"comments":[{"id":76,"issue_id":"bd-2nw.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Verification contract (mandatory): this bead must ship comprehensive unit tests, integration coverage, and E2E script coverage with detailed structured logging. Required logs include input metadata, algorithm or mode selection, diagnostics, degradation or fallback decisions where applicable, timing metrics, output hashes or sizes, and explicit pass fail reasons. CI artifacts must be sufficient for post-mortem debugging without rerun.","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:28:00Z"},{"id":95,"issue_id":"bd-2nw.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
1. Span Propagation Tests - verify span covers node/edge/subgraph declarations
2. Multi-span Tracking Tests - verify span_all collects all references
3. Layout Span Inheritance Tests - verify LayoutNode inherits IrNode spans

### Integration Tests
1. Full Pipeline Tests - Parse to Layout to Render with span assertions
2. Error Localization Tests - span points to exact error locations

### E2E Validation
1. Click-to-Source Tests - recover source span from rendered position
2. Unicode Tests - verify character-accurate boundaries

### Logging Requirements
- Structured JSON log entries with span fields
- Format: {stage, element_id, span: {start, end, line, col}}","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:28:13Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2nw.5","title":"Extract full terminal render capabilities from mermaid_render.rs","description":"# Terminal Render Full Extraction

Complete extraction of terminal rendering from FrankenTUI's mermaid_render.rs (9,352 lines) into fm-render-term.

## Reference Code
- Source: /dp/frankentui/crates/ftui-extras/src/mermaid_render.rs
- Lines: 9,352
- Purpose: Full-featured terminal diagram rendering

## Current State

fm-render-term has basic terminal rendering (~2,000 lines) but lacks:
- Full shape library (all 20+ node shapes)
- Advanced edge rendering (curves, routing)
- Style application (colors, bold, dim)
- Unicode box drawing optimization
- Cluster rendering with borders

## Key Features to Extract

### 1. Shape Rendering (all shapes)
- Rectangle, rounded-rect, circle, ellipse
- Diamond, hexagon, parallelogram, trapezoid
- Stadium, subroutine, cylinder, database
- Double-circle, flag, note, document
- All asymmetric shapes

### 2. Edge Rendering
- Straight edges with arrow heads
- Orthogonal routed edges
- Curved edges (quadratic Bézier approximation)
- Edge labels (inline, above, below)
- Multi-segment long edges

### 3. Text Rendering
- Unicode-aware text wrapping
- Grapheme clustering for width calculation
- Truncation with ellipsis
- Vertical centering in shapes
- Label overflow handling

### 4. Style System
- ANSI color support (16, 256, true color)
- Bold, italic, underline, dim
- Background colors
- Style inheritance (class-based)
- Theme support

### 5. Cluster Rendering
- Cluster borders (box drawing)
- Cluster titles
- Nested cluster support
- Cluster padding and margins

### 6. Performance Features
- Dirty region tracking
- Incremental redraw
- Virtual scrolling for large diagrams

## Implementation Notes

FrankenTUI uses:
- ftui-render trait system for drawing
- Cell-based canvas with dirty tracking
- Pre-computed glyph widths

We need to:
1. Create standalone RenderSurface trait
2. Implement TerminalSurface with crossterm/termion
3. Add mock surface for testing
4. Preserve rendering fidelity exactly

## Acceptance Criteria

- All 20+ node shapes rendered correctly
- Edge routing matches reference
- Style application complete
- Visual regression tests
- Performance: 60fps for 500-node diagrams","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:16:21.366755973Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T07:25:01.942317636Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T07:25:01.941457898Z","close_reason":"Terminal render capabilities extracted into fm-render-term: renderer.rs (943 lines) with TermRenderer, config.rs with fidelity tiers (Compact/Normal/Rich) and glyph modes (Unicode/ASCII), glyphs.rs with box-drawing characters, canvas.rs with sub-cell precision (Braille/Block/HalfBlock), ascii.rs with detection and normalization.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["extraction","render","terminal"],"comments":[{"id":75,"issue_id":"bd-2nw.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Verification contract (mandatory): this bead must ship comprehensive unit tests, integration coverage, and E2E script coverage with detailed structured logging. Required logs include input metadata, algorithm or mode selection, diagnostics, degradation or fallback decisions where applicable, timing metrics, output hashes or sizes, and explicit pass fail reasons. CI artifacts must be sufficient for post-mortem debugging without rerun.","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:28:00Z"},{"id":98,"issue_id":"bd-2nw.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (per shape)
Test each of 20+ node shapes:
- Rect, rounded-rect, circle, ellipse, diamond
- Hexagon, parallelogram, trapezoid, stadium
- Subroutine, cylinder, database, double-circle
- Flag, note, document, asymmetric shapes

### Edge Rendering Tests
1. Straight edge with all arrow types (-->, --o, --x, etc.)
2. Orthogonal routed edges with turns
3. Curved edges (Bezier approximation)
4. Edge labels (inline, above, below positions)
5. Multi-segment long edges spanning layers

### Integration Tests
1. Full diagram rendering with mock terminal surface
2. Determinism: same input = same terminal output
3. Unicode box drawing correctness
4. Cluster border rendering with nested content

### Visual Regression
Golden output files for each diagram type + shape combination

### Logging Requirements
- Trace-level logs for shape drawing decisions
- Per-shape render timing
- Character cell usage statistics","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:28:37Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2nw.6","title":"Implement adaptive quality degradation under compute pressure","description":"# Adaptive Quality Degradation under Compute Pressure (Parity Extension)

Goal:
- Validate and calibrate degradation behavior against reference expectations while using shared bd-3uz degradation infrastructure.

Scope:
- Calibrate pressure tiers, operator ordering, and quality scoring for parity corpus scenarios.
- Verify user facing explainability and quality mode behavior under load.

Verification and logging requirements:
- Unit tests for tier thresholds, operator scoring, and deterministic selection.
- Integration tests for parse, layout, render budget transitions.
- E2E stress scripts for CLI and WASM with repeat run determinism checks.
- Structured logs must include pressure signals, selected tier, operator chain, quality score, time saved, diagnostics, and pass fail reason.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:16:40.685241756Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-27T18:36:43.956551159Z","closed_at":"2026-03-27T18:36:43.956158976Z","close_reason":"Adaptive quality degradation under compute pressure is fully implemented. Budget broker allocates stage budgets by pressure tier (Nominal=250ms, Elevated=180ms, High=120ms, Critical=80ms). DegradationOperator library with 7 operators in canonical cost-order drives deterministic degradation plans. Guard report populated with real complexity/limit/budget data. All renderers consume degradation plan (SVG: shadows/gradients/glow, Term: glyph mode/tier/clusters/labels). CLI and WASM expose pressure diagnostics, budget usage, and degradation explanation. MermaidQualityMode exposes user quality-vs-latency controls. 33+ tests across fm-core, fm-layout, fm-render-svg, fm-render-term covering the full stack.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["degradation","performance","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.6","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.18","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:25:51.153473730Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.6","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:19:51.491688165Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.6","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:19:51.598196098Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.6","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:25:50.820655394Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.6","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:25:50.947665406Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.6","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:25:51.056688220Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":99,"issue_id":"bd-2nw.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
1. Pressure signal detection on native (CPU, memory thresholds)
2. Pressure tier quantization (low/medium/high/critical)
3. Quality degradation operator correctness
4. Budget allocation across pipeline stages

### Integration Tests
1. Full pipeline under simulated high load
2. Graceful degradation sequence verification
3. Quality vs latency tradeoff validation
4. Recovery when pressure subsides

### E2E Stress Tests
```bash
# scripts/test-degradation.sh
# Generate increasing load until degradation triggers
for size in 100 500 1000 5000 10000; do
  fm-cli render --max-budget-ms=100 large_diagram_${size}.mmd
  # Assert output quality monotonically decreases, never crashes
done
```

### Logging Requirements
- Structured pressure readings at each checkpoint
- Degradation operator invocation trace
- Quality score at each stage
- Total budget consumed vs allocated","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:28:42Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2nw.7","title":"Implement comprehensive determinism verification harness","description":"# Determinism Verification Harness (Parity Extension)

Goal:
- Prove deterministic behavior against both internal invariants and reference parity expectations.

Scope:
- Build parity drift classification and tolerance policies on top of shared proof harnesses.
- Produce cross platform determinism evidence packages for CI and release gates.

Verification and logging requirements:
- Unit tests for hash normalization and drift categorization logic.
- Integration tests for repeated parse, layout, render hash stability.
- E2E corpus replay across platforms with deterministic seeds.
- Structured logs must include run id, input hash, platform, stage hashes, drift class, tolerance rationale, and pass fail reason.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:16:57.189064124Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T04:30:31.479806803Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["determinism","testing","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2xl.13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:25:51.353827912Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.7","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:25:51.254202835Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":100,"issue_id":"bd-2nw.7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Testing Requirements

### Property-Based Tests (proptest)
1. Parse-layout-render roundtrip produces identical output
2. Re-running layout with same IR = same coordinates
3. Rendering same layout = same pixel/character output
4. Hash of output is stable across runs

### Determinism Proof Infrastructure
1. Golden test corpus with checksums
2. CI job that runs 100x and asserts identical output
3. Cross-platform determinism (Linux/macOS/WASM)

### Invariant Tests
1. Layout coordinates are always finite and bounded
2. No overlapping node bounding boxes (unless intentional)
3. Edge routes don't cross node interiors
4. Cluster boundaries contain all member nodes

### Replay Tests
1. Capture full trace of layout computation
2. Replay from trace produces identical result
3. Binary diffable trace format for debugging

### Logging Requirements
- JSON trace of all layout decisions
- Hash checkpoint at each pipeline stage
- Structured diff when non-determinism detected","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:28:51Z"}]}

Drift detection via FNV-1a checksum comparison.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["determinism","testing","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2xl.13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:25:51.353827912Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.7","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:25:51.254202835Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":100,"issue_id":"bd-2nw.7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Testing Requirements

### Property-Based Tests (proptest)
1. Parse-layout-render roundtrip produces identical output
2. Re-running layout with same IR = same coordinates
3. Rendering same layout = same pixel/character output
4. Hash of output is stable across runs

### Determinism Proof Infrastructure
1. Golden test corpus with checksums
2. CI job that runs 100x and asserts identical output
3. Cross-platform determinism (Linux/macOS/WASM)

### Invariant Tests
1. Layout coordinates are always finite and bounded
2. No overlapping node bounding boxes (unless intentional)
3. Edge routes don't cross node interiors
4. Cluster boundaries contain all member nodes

### Replay Tests
1. Capture full trace of layout computation
2. Replay from trace produces identical result
3. Binary diffable trace format for debugging

### Logging Requirements

- Hash checkpoint at each pipeline stage
- Structured diff when non-determinism detected","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:28:51Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2nw.8","title":"Build profile-first performance optimization suite with behavior proofs","description":"# Profile First Performance Optimization Suite (Parity Extension)

Goal:
- Compare performance against reference and drive optimization prioritization with behavior proof guarantees.

Scope:
- Reuse shared baseline and optimization waves from bd-3uz.
- Add parity focused performance deltas and hotspot attribution versus reference.

Verification and logging requirements:
- Unit tests for benchmark ingestion, normalization, and scoring.
- Integration tests for reproducible benchmark pipeline execution.
- E2E performance sweeps on representative and adversarial corpus segments.
- Structured logs must include p50 p95 p99 latency, throughput, memory proxy, hotspot ids, proof artifact links, and pass fail reason.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:17:14.500306875Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T04:30:31.636668099Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["optimization","performance","profiling"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.8","depends_on_id":"bd-2nw.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:19:59.610772627Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.8","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:25:51.468586936Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.8","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:25:51.580881435Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.8","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.14","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:25:51.696892032Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":101,"issue_id":"bd-2nw.8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Testing Requirements

### Profiling Infrastructure
1. Criterion benchmarks for parse/layout/render hot paths
2. CPU flame graph generation automation
3. Memory allocation profiling with dhat
4. WASM-specific profiling with browser DevTools

### Behavior Proof Requirements
For each optimization:
1. Before/after benchmark with statistical significance
2. Property test proving semantic equivalence
3. Determinism test proving output unchanged
4. Documentation of change and measured impact

### Regression Detection
1. Criterion historical comparison
2. Alert on >5% regression in any benchmark
3. CI gate on performance budgets

### E2E Performance Validation
```bash
# scripts/perf-validation.sh
hyperfine --warmup 3 'fm-cli render corpus/large.mmd'
# Assert p95 latency < 500ms for large diagrams
```

### Logging Requirements
- Structured timing data per stage
- Memory high-water mark tracking
- Hot path entry/exit tracing (compile-time optional)","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:28:57Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2nw.9","title":"Create comprehensive E2E validation pipeline with structured logging","description":"# Comprehensive E2E Validation Pipeline (Parity Extension)

Goal:
- Build end to end parity validation by composing shared CLI and WASM stress pipelines with reference comparison logic.

Scope:
- Reuse bd-3uz.22, bd-3uz.23, and bd-3uz.24 outputs and schemas.
- Add parity dimensions including semantic diff classes and visual diff signals where applicable.

Verification and logging requirements:
- Unit tests for comparator and report rollup logic.
- Integration tests for ingesting CLI and WASM telemetry into parity reports.
- E2E runs over normal, malformed recoverable, and adversarial corpus fixtures.
- Structured logs must include reference hash, FM hash, semantic diff class, visual diff score, timings, diagnostics, and pass fail reason.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:17:35.256287916Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T04:30:31.792893935Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","logging","testing","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.9","depends_on_id":"bd-2nw.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:19:51.397315569Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.9","depends_on_id":"bd-2nw.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:19:51.307477234Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.9","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.15","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:25:51.815323472Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.9","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.22","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:25:51.942479258Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.9","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.23","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:25:52.063898001Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.9","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.24","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:25:52.185815459Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":102,"issue_id":"bd-2nw.9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Testing Requirements

### E2E Test Scripts
```bash
# scripts/e2e-validation.sh

# Test all diagram types parse correctly
for type in flowchart sequence class state er gantt pie gitgraph; do
  fm-cli parse --validate corpus/${type}/*.mmd
done

# Test full pipeline
fm-cli render corpus/all/*.mmd --output=test_output/
diff -r test_output/ golden_output/

# Test error cases
fm-cli parse corpus/invalid/*.mmd --expected-errors
```

### Logging Infrastructure
1. Structured JSON logs at each pipeline stage
2. Log schema validation (jq-parseable)
3. Log aggregation for multi-file runs
4. Trace ID correlation across stages

### Coverage Requirements
1. Line coverage >90% for core modules
2. Branch coverage >80%
3. Integration test coverage for all CLI commands
4. Error path coverage for all recoverable errors

### CI Integration
1. E2E suite runs on every PR
2. Golden file updates require review
3. Log schema changes trigger alerts","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:29:06Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2puw","title":"Sequence: parse participant stereotype/config declarations and preserve actor glyph shape intent","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-23T19:58:31.951426301Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-23T20:05:44.613586020Z","closed_at":"2026-03-23T20:05:44.613091624Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parity","sequence"]}

 {"id":"bd-2xl.10","title":"Create E2E test script with full pipeline validation and detailed logging","description":"Create scripts/e2e_test.sh (modeled on DCG's e2e_test.sh) that exercises the complete FrankenMermaid pipeline end-to-end. The script must:

1. TEST CORPUS: Ship a tests/e2e_corpus/ directory with .mmd files covering every diagram type. At least 3 files per type: simple, complex, edge-case. Include malformed inputs for error recovery testing.

2. PER-FILE TEST PIPELINE: For each .mmd file:
   a. Run fm-cli render --format svg -- verify exit code 0, SVG output is non-empty and valid XML
   b. Run fm-cli render --format term -- verify exit code 0, output contains box-drawing characters
   c. Run fm-cli parse --format json -- verify exit code 0, JSON output parses, has nodes array
   d. Run fm-cli detect -- verify exit code 0, detected type matches expected type
   e. If golden SVG exists in tests/e2e_golden/, compare output hash

3. ERROR RECOVERY TESTS: For each malformed input:
   a. Verify fm-cli does NOT exit with error (exit 0)
   b. Verify output contains diagnostic warnings
   c. Verify SVG output is still produced (best-effort)

4. PERFORMANCE BUDGET: Time each render. Report median/p95/max. Fail if any single render exceeds 5 seconds.

5. DETAILED LOGGING: Every test case prints:
   - Input file name and size
   - Detected diagram type and confidence
   - Node count, edge count, cluster count
   - SVG output size (bytes)
   - Render time (ms)
   - PASS/FAIL status with specific reason on failure
   At end: summary table with pass/fail counts per diagram type.

6. OUTPUT: JSON report in e2e_output.json with all test results. Exit code 0 if all pass, 1 if any fail.

7. CI INTEGRATION: --ci flag for machine-readable output. --verbose flag for debug-level logging. --filter flag to run subset of tests.

Target: 100+ individual test cases across all diagram types.

## Acceptance Criteria

- All functionality explicitly described above is implemented fully; no scope reduction, no feature deletions, and no silent behavioral regressions.
- Code is modular, documented where needed, and integrated with existing crates/interfaces without compatibility shims or duplicated logic.
- Comprehensive automated tests are added/updated for this issue: focused unit tests + relevant integration coverage for touched behavior and edge cases.
- End-to-end validation is covered where applicable (CLI and/or WASM path) with detailed structured logging (inputs, detection results, node/edge counts, output sizes, timings, and pass/fail reasons).
- Diagnostics are actionable: failures/warnings include precise context and suggested remediation.
- Determinism and stability expectations are verified for affected outputs (layout/render/serialization as applicable).

 {"id":"bd-2xl.11","title":"Write cross-crate integration tests with tracing instrumentation","description":"Create tests/integration/ directory with cross-crate integration tests that exercise the full pipeline: parse -> layout -> render. Each test uses tracing-subscriber with EnvFilter to enable per-module logging controllable via RUST_LOG env var.

Test scenarios:
1. ROUNDTRIP: parse mermaid string, layout, render SVG, parse SVG XML, verify node/edge counts match.
2. DETERMINISM: same input through full pipeline 10 times, verify byte-identical SVG output.
3. LARGE GRAPH: 500-node flowchart, verify completes within 2 seconds, no panic, valid SVG.
4. ALL DIAGRAM TYPES: one integration test per diagram type that goes through full pipeline.
5. ERROR PROPAGATION: malformed input produces SVG with diagnostic annotations (not empty SVG).
6. THEME SWITCHING: same diagram rendered with all themes produces valid but distinct SVGs.
7. CONFIG OVERRIDE: mermaid-js style config object correctly affects output.

Each test must use #[tracing::instrument] and emit structured log events at key pipeline stages:
- parse_start / parse_complete (with node_count, edge_count, diagnostics_count)
- layout_start / layout_complete (with algorithm, iterations, crossings, elapsed_ms)
- render_start / render_complete (with output_format, byte_size, element_count)

This enables debugging via: RUST_LOG=fm_parser=debug,fm_layout=trace cargo test integration

Target: 30+ integration tests.

## Acceptance Criteria

- All functionality explicitly described above is implemented fully; no scope reduction, no feature deletions, and no silent behavioral regressions.
- Code is modular, documented where needed, and integrated with existing crates/interfaces without compatibility shims or duplicated logic.
- Comprehensive automated tests are added/updated for this issue: focused unit tests + relevant integration coverage for touched behavior and edge cases.
- End-to-end validation is covered where applicable (CLI and/or WASM path) with detailed structured logging (inputs, detection results, node/edge counts, output sizes, timings, and pass/fail reasons).
- Diagnostics are actionable: failures/warnings include precise context and suggested remediation.
- Determinism and stability expectations are verified for affected outputs (layout/render/serialization as applicable).
- Quality gates pass: `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo check --all-targets`, and relevant `cargo test` scope.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T18:19:21.699306618Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-27T20:24:34.455466615Z","closed_at":"2026-03-27T20:24:34.455200001Z","close_reason":"88 cross-crate integration tests in fm-cli (integration_test.rs) covering parse→layout→render pipeline for all diagram types, plus golden layout checksums (23 cases × 10 runs), golden SVG snapshots, invariant proof harness (12 tests), cross-target E2E tests, and layout quality benchmarks. Tracing instrumented throughout pipeline via tracing crate.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["integration","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2xl.11","depends_on_id":"bd-106","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2xl.11","depends_on_id":"bd-1y5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2xl.11","depends_on_id":"bd-2xl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2xl.11","depends_on_id":"bd-3bc.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2xl.11","depends_on_id":"bd-vb9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2xl.12","title":"Build differential mermaid-js compatibility and perceptual diff harness","description":"Create a differential compatibility harness that compares FrankenMermaid output against mermaid-js behavior at scale.

Scope:
1. Build a comparator pipeline for parse success/failure class, detected diagram type, and rendered output semantics.
2. Add perceptual SVG diff checks (not only hash equality) with tunable tolerance.
3. Record compatibility deltas per diagram type and syntax feature.
4. Generate HTML+JSON reports with failing fixtures, diff artifacts, and likely root-cause tags.
5. Wire regression budgets into CI to prevent compatibility backsliding.

Why this improves the system:
- Gives objective evidence for migration readiness and highlights true user-facing gaps quickly.

-{"id":"bd-2xl.13","title":"Establish cross-platform determinism matrix and drift detector","description":"Guarantee deterministic rendering behavior across Linux/macOS/Windows and common architecture targets.

Scope:
1. Add deterministic snapshot jobs on a cross-platform CI matrix.
2. Normalize known nondeterministic metadata fields from SVG outputs before comparison.
3. Run repeated render loops to detect nondeterministic drift over N iterations.
4. Publish deterministic fingerprints (hashes) as CI artifacts for triage.
5. Fail CI on unexplained output drift with artifact links.

Why this improves the system:
- Protects one of the project's core promises: identical input yields identical output.

 {"id":"bd-2xl.14","title":"Build automated failing-input minimizer for reproducible bug triage","description":"Implement an automatic failing-input minimizer to shrink reproduction cases for parser/layout/render bugs.

Scope:
1. Add reducer that minimizes .mmd inputs while preserving target failure signature.
2. Support failure signatures: panic, timeout, invalid output class, deterministic mismatch.
3. Integrate reducer into fuzz and adversarial test workflows.
4. Store minimized repro artifacts with trace bundles for triage.
5. Provide CLI utility command for manual reduction sessions.

Why this improves the system:
- Dramatically reduces debugging time for hard edge-case failures.

 {"id":"bd-2xl.15","title":"Add real-world Mermaid corpus ingestion and trend analysis pipeline","description":"Create a real-world corpus ingestion pipeline from public README mermaid snippets for compatibility tracking.

Scope:
1. Build a curated, license-safe corpus collector for Mermaid snippets from public repositories.
2. Normalize and deduplicate corpus inputs by diagram type and syntax profile.
3. Run nightly compatibility and rendering checks against corpus.
4. Publish trend reports: pass rate, warning rate, top failure signatures.
5. Feed high-value failures into regression fixture sets automatically.

Why this improves the system:
- Aligns roadmap priorities with real user inputs, not synthetic examples only.

 {"id":"bd-2xl.2","title":"Implement property-based tests with proptest","description":"Port and expand the proptest suites from FrankenTUI (proptest_mermaid_parser_invariants, proptest_mermaid_layout_invariants, proptest_mermaid_render_invariants). Properties to verify:

Parser invariants: (1) parse never panics for any input string, (2) parse always returns IR (never Err, per error recovery design), (3) re-serializing IR and re-parsing produces equivalent IR (round-trip where applicable), (4) detected diagram type is consistent across multiple calls.

Layout invariants: (1) layout never panics, (2) all nodes have non-overlapping bounding boxes (within configurable tolerance), (3) all clusters contain their child nodes, (4) layout is deterministic (same input = same output, byte-identical), (5) quality metrics are non-negative.

Render invariants: (1) SVG output is valid XML, (2) SVG contains expected number of node/edge elements, (3) render never panics, (4) terminal render fits within specified dimensions.

## Acceptance Criteria

- All functionality explicitly described above is implemented fully; no scope reduction, no feature deletions, and no silent behavioral regressions.
- Code is modular, documented where needed, and integrated with existing crates/interfaces without compatibility shims or duplicated logic.

- End-to-end validation is covered where applicable (CLI and/or WASM path) with detailed structured logging (inputs, detection results, node/edge counts, output sizes, timings, and pass/fail reasons).
- Diagnostics are actionable: failures/warnings include precise context and suggested remediation.
- Determinism and stability expectations are verified for affected outputs (layout/render/serialization as applicable).
- Quality gates pass: `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo check --all-targets`, and relevant `cargo test` scope.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T16:36:55.989744262Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T07:20:14.811901324Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T07:20:14.811169989Z","close_reason":"Added 10 new proptests across 3 crates (fm-parser: 3, fm-layout: 3, fm-render-svg: 4). SVG proptests: output determinism, all themes, viewBox presence, no NaN/Infinity. Total: 419 tests across 3 crates (215+98+106). All pass, clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["proptest","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2xl.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2xl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2xl.2","depends_on_id":"bd-3bc.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
